operating_state: Net sales increased 4.9% to $45.3 billion in Q2 2025, driven primarily by SRS acquisition contributing incremental revenue. Comparable sales rose 1.0% reflecting 1.4% higher average ticket offset by 0.4% decline in customer transactions. Performance reflects customer engagement with smaller home improvement projects pressured by persistent high interest rates constraining large project demand.
cost_structure: Gross margin held flat at 33.4% as Primary segment benefits from shrink and supply chain efficiencies offset SRS inclusion. SG&A increased to 17.1% of sales from 16.5% due to higher payroll costs in Primary segment. Depreciation and amortization rose driven by increased intangible asset amortization from SRS acquisition.
material_events: Entered definitive agreement to acquire GMS for $110 per share representing $4.3 billion equity value, expected to close second half fiscal 2025 funded through cash and commercial paper. Completed $2.0 billion senior notes offering in September 2025 with tranches maturing 2028, 2030, and 2035. Increased quarterly dividend 2.2% to $2.30 per share and repaid $1.2 billion long-term debt during first six months.
macro_exposures: Continue monitoring tariff and trade policy developments with ongoing supply chain diversification efforts to maintain customer value proposition. Stronger U.S. dollar negatively impacted net sales by $163 million in Q2 and $437 million in first six months. High interest rate environment persists, pressuring large home improvement project demand while supporting smaller project engagement.
forward_outlook: Expect approximately $4 billion capital expenditures in fiscal 2025, representing 2.5% of net sales, focused on customer experience technology, Pro capabilities, and new stores. GMS acquisition expected to close second half fiscal 2025 subject to regulatory approvals. Share repurchases remain paused through fiscal 2025 following SRS acquisition financing.
uncertainty_profile: GMS acquisition closing contingent on regulatory approvals and majority shareholder tender. Commercial paper program expanded to $11.0 billion supported by equivalent backup credit facilities to accommodate acquisition financing. OBBBA tax law changes expected to reduce fiscal 2025 cash tax payments through enhanced expensing provisions.
